agent_hints.inputs: DataFrame with string columns to normalize.
agent_hints.outputs: Same columns; string values trimmed and case-normalized in the specified `columns`.
agent_hints.side_effects: Modifies VALUES (not names) IN PLACE. IMPORTANT: if `columns` is omitted or empty, normalize_case applies to ALL string columns INCLUDING target/label columns — a downstream `filter` condition like "Survived == 'Yes'" will then match ZERO rows because values are now 'yes'. Either list `columns` explicitly (excluding labels) or match the normalized case in downstream filters.
agent_hints.anti_uses: Not for renaming columns. Not for schema coercion (use type_coercer). Do NOT omit `columns` if you plan to filter on a string label column downstream.

agent_hints.outputs: Same columns; PII values redacted/hashed/tokenized per strategy.
agent_hints.side_effects: Modifies values IN PLACE. Original values IRRETRIEVABLE if strategy=hash/redact.
agent_hints.anti_uses: Config shape: rules=[{column: <col>, method: <hash|partial|redact|pseudonymize>, ...}]. The rule key is `method` NOT `strategy`. Valid methods: 'hash', 'partial' (keep_last N chars), 'redact' (replace with fixed string), 'pseudonymize' (deterministic surrogate). Not for schema-level column drops (use select_columns). Not reversible with hash/redact.

components.name: Database Replication
components.description: Replicate a table between two SQL databases (Oracle/Db2/Postgres/MySQL/MSSQL → Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift/Databricks/etc.) without landing rows in Python memory. Wraps Sling under the hood via the official dagster-sling integration. Supports full_refresh / incremental / snapshot / truncate modes with upsert via primary_key.

agent_hints.inputs: None (root ingestion) — reads from a source DB, writes to a target DB.
agent_hints.outputs: None (sink-like — Sling handles both ends). Emits materialization events with row counts.
agent_hints.side_effects: Copies data from source_connection_env_var to target. Mode: full_refresh / incremental / truncate.
agent_hints.anti_uses: For one-time migration inventory use database_schema_inventory. For DDL migration use database_tables_migration. For DataFrame in-memory analytics use dataframe_from_sql.

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Database Schema Inventory
components.description: List every database object (tables/views/procedures/functions/sequences/triggers/scheduled jobs) in a source SQL DB as a DataFrame. Companion to database_replication for warehouse-migration planning. Supports postgres/mysql/mssql/oracle/db2/snowflake/redshift.

agent_hints.inputs: None (root source) — introspects DB metadata via connection_env_var.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with columns: object_type, schema_name, object_name, definition, row_count. One row per DB object.
agent_hints.side_effects: Read-only. Used for warehouse-migration scoping.
agent_hints.anti_uses: Not for data extraction (use database_query). Not for schema comparison across DBs (that's manual review of the inventory).

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Database Tables Migration
components.description: DDL-first warehouse migration: build portable CREATE TABLE statements from source INFORMATION_SCHEMA (types + PKs + FKs + NOT NULL + DEFAULT + CHECK + UNIQUE) and execute on target. Pre-flight warnings for non-enforcing targets (Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift). Status DataFrame per table.

agent_hints.inputs: None (root) — copies table DDL from source to target DB.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with per-table migration status (schema_name, table_name, target_table, status, ddl_preview).
agent_hints.side_effects: Creates tables on TARGET DB. Idempotent with drop_if_exists=true. Requires source + target database_connection.
agent_hints.anti_uses: For DATA copy use database_replication. For view migration use database_views_migration.

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Database View Migration
components.description: Migrate a single SQL view from source to target with table-ref + function-name substitutions. Reads source DDL, rewrites refs, executes CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW on target. Composable — one YAML per view.

agent_hints.inputs: None (root) — migrates a single view from source to target.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with single-row status.
agent_hints.side_effects: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW on target.

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Database Views Migration
components.description: Bulk-migrate every view in a source schema to a target with shared substitutions. Emits a per-view status DataFrame (schema_name, view_name, status, error_message) for the migration completion report.

agent_hints.inputs: None (root) — bulk view migration.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with per-view status.
agent_hints.side_effects: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW for each. Requires source + target database_connection.

components.name: Filter
components.description: Filter rows from a DataFrame using a query condition.

agent_hints.inputs: DataFrame with columns referenced by the condition.
agent_hints.outputs: Same columns; fewer rows. Preserves column order and dtypes.
agent_hints.side_effects: Reduces row count; column set unchanged. If the condition matches 0 rows, the step succeeds but writes an EMPTY output — check upstream data values (case, whitespace, type) when this happens.
agent_hints.anti_uses: condition is a Python-style expression evaluated with pandas eval. Column names must exist in upstream. Value CASE matters: if `data_cleansing` with normalize_case=lower ran upstream, string values are now lowercase — use `condition: "Survived == 'yes'"` (not 'Yes'). For case-insensitive match, use `Survived.str.lower() == 'yes'`.

agent_hints.anti_uses: expressions is Dict[str, str] where each value is a pandas eval expression (e.g. `{month: order_date.dt.month}`, `{is_high: (total > 500).astype(int)}`). components.name: Sentry Alert Job
components.description: Fire a Sentry event as a scheduled (or on-demand) job — no asset materialized. Matches the shape of http_webhook_job / observability_heartbeat_job. Configurable message ({now} substitution), level, environment/release, Sentry tags/extras/fingerprint. Common uses: nightly heartbeat that on-call watches for gaps; release-notification events; scheduled 'job completed' signals into the Sentry inbox.

agent_hints.inputs: None — schedule-triggered or on-demand.
agent_hints.outputs: None (op job — sends one Sentry event per run, no asset materialized).
agent_hints.side_effects: Requires SENTRY_DSN env var + sentry-sdk installed. Message supports {now} placeholder for UTC ISO timestamp.
agent_hints.anti_uses: For per-row events from a DataFrame use dataframe_to_sentry (asset sink). For reading issues use sentry_issues_ingestion. For generic webhook use http_webhook_job. For pinging any observability endpoint use observability_heartbeat_job.

components.name: Sentry Issues Ingestion
components.description: Pull issues from a Sentry project via the /api/0/projects REST API. Uses Link-header cursor pagination. Returns a pandas DataFrame with flattened issue fields (id, shortId, title, level, status, count, userCount, firstSeen, lastSeen, project.*, metadata.*).

agent_hints.inputs: None (root ingestion) — reads issues from a Sentry project.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with issue fields (id, shortId, title, level, status, count, userCount, firstSeen, lastSeen, permalink).
agent_hints.side_effects: Read-only. Requires SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN with `project:read` scope. Cursor-paginated via Link header. Query language matches Sentry's search bar (e.g. 'is:unresolved', 'level:error age:-24h').
agent_hints.anti_uses: For sending events TO Sentry use dataframe_to_sentry. For querying events (individual occurrences) not issues (aggregated), use a different endpoint. Not for real-time — poll on a schedule.

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Sentry Monitor Check-in Job
components.description: Register a Dagster scheduled job as a Sentry Cron Monitor. On each run, fires an in_progress check-in at start and an ok check-in at end. If Sentry doesn't see the expected check-in within the schedule window + checkin_margin, it alerts on-call. Optional monitor_config auto-creates the monitor on first check-in. Matches the pattern of http_webhook_job / observability_heartbeat_job. Complements sentry_alert_job (which fires arbitrary events on schedule).

agent_hints.inputs: None — schedule-triggered or on-demand.
agent_hints.outputs: None (op job — sends one Sentry cron check-in per run: in_progress at start, ok at end).
agent_hints.side_effects: Requires SENTRY_DSN env var + sentry-sdk>=1.30. If monitor_config is set, auto-creates/updates the monitor on first check-in. Default fail_on_error=False so a Sentry outage doesn't break the scheduler.
agent_hints.anti_uses: For an ARBITRARY Sentry event (message) on schedule use sentry_alert_job — different concept (alerts inbox, not cron monitor). For generic webhook pings use http_webhook_job. For per-row events from a DataFrame use dataframe_to_sentry.

For ANY 'group by X, sum/count/mean Y' task — USE summarize, not count_records + formula. count_records only gives a grand total; formula only computes per-row values — neither does group-by aggregation. If upstream was one_hot_encoded, branch off pre-encoded upstream.

components.name: Weighted Average
components.description: Compute a weighted average of a column, optionally grouped.

agent_hints.inputs: DataFrame with a value column + weight column + optional group_by.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame with weighted-avg per group.
agent_hints.side_effects: Reduces to one row per group. Column set changes.
agent_hints.anti_uses: For unweighted mean use summarize with agg=mean.

components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: WindowCalculationComponent
components.description: Window functions — PARTITION BY + ORDER BY + (lag, lead, rank, dense_rank, row_number, cumulative sum, moving avg). The full SQL window-function shape, in pandas.
components.author: Dagster Community

agent_hints.inputs: DataFrame with a numeric column + optional partition/order columns.
agent_hints.outputs: DataFrame + new column with the window function result (rolling mean, lag, lead, cumulative sum, etc.).
agent_hints.side_effects: Adds one new column. NaNs appear at partition boundaries depending on window function.
agent_hints.anti_uses: Config shape: order_by (list of columns), partition_by (optional list), operations=[...]. Each operation dict: {output: <new_col>, func: <one of row_number|rank|dense_rank|lag|lead|cumsum|cummax|cummin|moving_avg|moving_sum>, column: <SOURCE_col> (NOT 'col'), periods: N (lag/lead only), window: N (moving_* only)}. Key names are EXACT: `output`, `func`, `column`, `periods`, `window`. Do NOT abbreviate to `col` — schema requires `column`. For simple pct_change use pct_change instead. For groupwise aggregations use summarize.

components.name: Qlik Replicate Resource

agent_hints.anti_uses: Do not use for Fivetran, Airbyte, or dagster-native replication — those have dedicated components. Use only when the customer already runs Qlik Enterprise Manager as their Replicate control plane.

components.name: Qlik Replicate Task Trigger Job
components.description: Trigger a Qlik Replicate task (run/stop/reload) via the Enterprise Manager REST API. Wraps a Dagster job around a single Replicate control-plane call. For continuous CDC use wait_for_completion=false; for one-shot migrations set true so the job waits for terminal state.
components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Qlik Replicate Task Status Sensor
components.description: Poll a Qlik Replicate task's state via Enterprise Manager REST API; trigger a Dagster job on any transition into a target state (default STOPPED). Event-driven pattern for chaining downstream work off Replicate task completion or failure.
components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Qlik Replicate Task Metrics Ingestion
components.description: Poll Qlik Enterprise Manager for per-task metrics (state, latency, throughput, error count) and emit a DataFrame asset. One row per task per materialization. Foundation for CDC observability dashboards and alert-input assets.
components.author: Dagster Community

components.name: Qlik Replicate Workspace
components.description: Auto-emit one Dagster asset per Qlik Replicate task by enumerating the workspace via the Qlik Enterprise Manager REST API. StateBackedComponent — discovery is cached to disk and refreshed on explicit trigger. Materializing an asset triggers the underlying task (run / reload / stop) and polls to completion. The workspace-shape peer of the four low-level qlik_replicate_* components — use this when you want one YAML instead of one-per-task.
components.author: Dagster Community

agent_hints.anti_uses: Do not use alongside per-task qlik_replicate_task_trigger_job unless a specific task needs bespoke config outside the workspace defaults. This workspace already emits per-task assets.
